title: Athyrium uropteron
intro: Athyrium uropteron, also known as the lady fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Woodsiaceae. The health benefits of Athyrium uropteron include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to traditional medicine. The most common herbal preparations made with Athyrium uropteron include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Athyrium uropteron is classified within the family Woodsiaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. It belongs to the genus Athyrium, which comprises a variety of species of ferns. The species Athyrium uropteron is native to East Asia, and its classification reflects its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the region. Within the family Woodsiaceae, Athyrium uropteron is part of the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are characterized by their complex leaf structures.
